message:
fix(core,compat): preserve DEFAULT parentheses in DDL, complete compat persist

Two fixes for SQLite compatibility mode:

1. format_default_value() now preserves parentheses for ParenExpr:
   `DEFAULT (strftime(...))` was rendered as `DEFAULT strftime(...)`
   which real SQLite rejects with "near '(': syntax error".

2. build_create_table_sql() in compat_persist now emits complete
   column constraints: NOT NULL, UNIQUE, DEFAULT, COLLATE, and
   GENERATED ALWAYS AS. Previously these were all silently dropped,
   producing incomplete DDL that couldn't round-trip with real SQLite.

Also adds tests for PreparedStatement DML (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE) via
execute_with_params() covering the fix from GH-13.

Closes #14

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
